markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games has locked Grand Theft Auto VI for a November 19, 2026 release following two official delays from the original 2025 target and a brief May 2026 window, citing the need for additional polish. This timeline sets the stage for trader focus on pre-launch catalysts such as new trailers, marketing pushes, or further delay signals ahead of Take-Two Interactive’s next earnings call. Industry observers note Rockstar’s pattern of prioritizing quality over speed, with recent employee morale issues and union-related protests adding uncertainty to development momentum. Upcoming earnings updates and any surprise promotional drops could shift expectations around chart performance, streaming tie-ins, or ancillary content releases before the open-world sequel arrives.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent mistrial in Harvey Weinstein's New York retrial on the Jessica Mann allegations, declared May 15, 2026 after a deadlocked jury, has left that charge unresolved pending a June 24 hearing on any fourth trial. Traders appear to weigh this against his existing 16-year California sentence from the 2022 conviction, which he is actively appealing, alongside documented health issues including cancer, diabetes, and mobility limitations requiring a wheelchair. At age 74 and having been incarcerated since 2020, these factors contribute to market-implied odds favoring limited or no additional prison time beyond current commitments, as appeals processes and potential compassionate considerations introduce substantial uncertainty in entertainment industry legal outcomes. Upcoming sentencing on the 2025 New York conviction for the Miriam Haley count and any California appeal rulings remain key near-term catalysts.

markets.question: Will the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vegas Golden Knights hold a 2-1 lead in the 2026 Stanley Cup Final after a 5-4 double-overtime victory in Game 3 on June 6, fueled by Mitch Marner’s hat trick and Shea Theodore’s winner, shifting trader consensus toward their 63.7% implied probability. Carolina Hurricanes, who posted the NHL’s best regular-season record at 53-22-7 with 113 points, responded with a Game 2 overtime win but trail after dropping the opener on the road. Vegas finished the regular season at 39-26-17, topping the Pacific Division before advancing with strong defensive showings en route to the Final. The current series positioning and recent momentum in high-stakes games underpin the market’s valuation of each club’s championship prospects.

events.title: 2026 NBA Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NBA Finals.

eventMetadata.context_description: The New York Knicks hold an overwhelming edge in the 2026 NBA champion market after seizing a 2-0 lead in the Finals with road victories over the San Antonio Spurs. Their defensive versatility, particularly in containing Victor Wembanyama through physical frontcourt play and perimeter length, has disrupted San Antonio's offense in both games, while New York's balanced attack led by Jalen Brunson and Karl-Anthony Towns has capitalized on late execution. The Spurs entered the series as favorites but have seen momentum shift following a narrow Game 2 defeat and an earlier 11-game winning streak snapped by the Knicks. Traders have priced this recent series dominance into heavy implied probability for New York, though the Spurs retain realistic comeback potential if they adjust defensively at home or exploit any Knicks fatigue in subsequent games.

eventMetadata.context_description: The New York Knicks hold an overwhelming edge in the 2026 NBA champion market after seizing a 2-0 lead in the Finals with road victories over the San Antonio Spurs. Their defensive versatility, particularly in containing Victor Wembanyama through physical frontcourt play and perimeter length, has disrupted San Antonio's offense in both games, while New York's balanced attack led by Jalen Brunson and Karl-Anthony Towns has capitalized on late execution. The Spurs entered the series as favorites but have seen momentum shift following a narrow Game 2 defeat and an earlier 11-game winning streak snapped by the Knicks. Traders have priced this recent series dominance into heavy implied probability for New York, though the Spurs retain realistic comeback potential if they adjust defensively at home or exploit any Knicks fatigue in subsequent games.

markets.question: Will Spain win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping's consolidated control over the Chinese Communist Party, state institutions, and military underpins the strong trader consensus against his removal before the end of 2026. No verified internal challenges, health crises, or elite realignments have surfaced in 2026 despite earlier speculation around military personnel changes. Recent public appearances, including a May 2026 summit with U.S. President Trump in Beijing and March directives advancing health policy priorities, signal uninterrupted leadership. Analysts highlight the absence of any designated successor and widespread expectations that Xi will pursue a fourth term at the 21st Party Congress in autumn 2027, which further stabilizes his position through the market's resolution window. Any departure would require unprecedented events such as sudden illness or factional upheaval, neither of which has occurred.

markets.question: Will Gavin Newsom win the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

events.title: Democratic Presidential Nominee 2028
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

eventMetadata.context_description: Following the 2024 election defeat, the Democratic presidential field for 2028 has stayed fragmented with no dominant frontrunner, as shown by the spread of probabilities across dozens of names. Gavin Newsom leads at 25.1% on the strength of his California governorship, national fundraising network, and repeated media visibility that positions him for early organizational advantages. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez at 9.2% draws from progressive activists, Kamala Harris at 7.8% retains residual name recognition from her prior nomination, and Senate or gubernatorial figures such as Jon Ossoff and Josh Shapiro sit just behind. Differentiators center on ideological balance, donor access, and perceived electability in battleground states. Consolidation would likely require strong primary performances, endorsements from party leaders, or shifts in polling within key demographics before formal voting begins.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Following the 2024 election defeat, the Democratic presidential field for 2028 has stayed fragmented with no dominant frontrunner, as shown by the spread of probabilities across dozens of names. Gavin Newsom leads at 25.1% on the strength of his California governorship, national fundraising network, and repeated media visibility that positions him for early organizational advantages. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez at 9.2% draws from progressive activists, Kamala Harris at 7.8% retains residual name recognition from her prior nomination, and Senate or gubernatorial figures such as Jon Ossoff and Josh Shapiro sit just behind. Differentiators center on ideological balance, donor access, and perceived electability in battleground states. Consolidation would likely require strong primary performances, endorsements from party leaders, or shifts in polling within key demographics before formal voting begins.
